id: Threads of Harmony: An Evening With Vocal FX and Friends-8 Duncan St, Tawa, Wellington 5028, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Threads of Harmony: An Evening With Vocal FX and Friends
venue: 8 Duncan St, Tawa, Wellington 5028, New Zealand
long_description:
Vocal FX is a group spanning over 20 years of growth, success, and change. This concert is inspired by the themes of the 2025 National Barbershop Convention He Awa Whiria, weaving the braids of connection within our barbershop community like a river braid.
Throughout its history, Vocal FX has sewn threads of connection far and wide; we plan to highlight some of these relationships across the evening, while acknowledging the changes we are making to who we are, from within.
This concert will feature songs acknowledging the past, recognising new beginnings, and looking into the future, with who we strive to become.
Thank you to everyone new and old on this journey with us, we are looking forward to performing with, and for, all of you.

id: Opening Day: Messengers Artists’ Talk-Corner Molesworth and Aitken Streets, Thorndon, Wellington-Facebook
name: Opening Day: Messengers Artists’ Talk
venue: Corner Molesworth and Aitken Streets, Thorndon, Wellington
long_description:
Join us to celebrate the opening of City Gallery Wellington Te Whare Toi’s exhibition Messengers at The National Library of New Zealand Te Puna Mātauranga o Aotearoa. Hear artists Denise Batchelor, Brit Bunkley, Jane Dodd and John Ward Knox in a series of conversations with curator Megan Dunn, about how each artists work ask us to consider the ‘unspeaking companionship’ between humanity and other animals.
Denise Batchelor is a visual artist based in Hokianga, in the far north of New Zealand. Batchelor has an MFA (2010) from Whitecliffe College of Arts and Design, Auckland. Working primarily in digital media, both still and moving image, Batchelor’s work reflects personal encounters within nature; quiet moments of reflection within which deeper connections can be experienced. She has exhibited widely in New Zealand for the past fifteen years, and internationally in Sweden, Germany, Italy, USA, Scotland and France. A recipient of artist residencies and art awards, her work is held in public and private collections.
Brit Bunkley is a New Zealand-based artist and videographer whose practice spans sculpture, installation, and digital media. A recipient of the US National Endowment for the Arts fellowship, two NY state grants, and the Rome Prize Fellowship, his work explores the intersection of physical and virtual spaces, often blending surreal imagery with themes of history, politics, and environmental concerns. He has exhibited at the Museum of New Zealand Te Papa and internationally at Rencontres Internationales Paris/Berlin, FILE São Paulo (including 2025), and the Athens Digital Arts Festival. Recent exhibitions include Visions in the Nunnery, London (2022), Cybersculpture 2024 at Collège des Bernardins, Paris, and The Torrance Art Museum, LA (2025). In 2024, he won Best Art | Experimental Video/Film at the New York City Independent Film Festival. His work continues to evolve, embracing new technologies and alternative methodologies to challenge perceptions of reality and imagination.
Jane Dodd is a contemporary jeweller based in Dunedin. Dodd has a BA from Otago University and a Diploma in 3D design from UNITEC in Auckland. Since the early ‘90s she has exhibited widely in solo and group exhibitions in New Zealand and internationally.
Her jewellery is largely a carving-based practice utilizing re-purposed woods and bone. Narratives from the environment and animal world, homages to historic crafts, critiques of modern society, mysterious plotlines, comedy and tragedy can all be found in her jewellery.
John Ward Knox is an artist living in Karitane, north of Ōtepoti (Dunedin). He has a Master of Fine Arts from Elam School of Fine Arts and was the recipient of the National Drawing Award, in 2008, and the Frances Hodgkins Fellowship at Otago University, in 2015. His work has been included in The Archibald Prize, Art Gallery of New South Wales, Sydney (2020) and is included in New Zealand and Australian collections. Ward Knox leans towards materials and objects that are imbued with time and patience and are rewarding to touch.

long_description:
Carmichael are back at it again, with their monthly instalment at the Rogue and Vagabond. Joined by very special guests & friends, come escape the final days of hōtoke with one hell of a dance party!
Carmichael is a six-piece jazz ensemble, based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. Inspired by different global groove-driven collectives, such as Kokoroko, Ezra Collective, Nubiyan Twist & Fat Freddy’s Drop, their music blends elements of afrobeat, neo-soul and jazz fusion.
Featuring the beloved faces of the below Carmichaelians:
Lily Atu - alto sax & vocals
Elisa Bird - baritone sax
Frank Paton - trumpet
Darcy Monteath - bass
Petar Tavich - guitar
Joseph Alexander - drums
& special guests to be announced soon!

long_description:
ArtSplash is a week-long festival, held every two years, that celebrates tāmariki and rangatahi experiencing performance on the big stage in dance and choir concerts. This year, we have two separate groups performing – one at ArtSplash and the other at DanceSplash.
Whaea Emilee and Mrs Skippers have an amazing Choir group training to perform on Monday 25 August at the 5pm show, held at the Michael Fowler Centre.
This group will be heading into town by 8:15am and with an action-packed day planned, will stay in town until their 5pm performance.
Following the show, our students will be ready to head home with their spectator parents. Watch this space for more details and how you can help.
In the meantime, follow the link to book tickets on the Ticketmaster website– don’t miss out!

id: ELIXIR CLOSING Blindfolded Daiquiri Competition VII x WCC x Diplomatico Rum-8 Chews Ln, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: ELIXIR CLOSING Blindfolded Daiquiri Competition VII x WCC x Diplomatico Rum
venue: 8 Chews Ln, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
𝐈𝐭’𝐬 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐥𝐚𝐬𝐭 𝐜𝐚𝐥𝐥, 𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐬 — 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐰𝐞’𝐫𝐞 𝐠𝐨𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐨𝐮𝐭 𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐚 𝐛𝐚𝐧𝐠.
For the SEVENTH consecutive year, we're bringing back our legendary Blindfolded Daiquiri Competition and YOU are all invited!
Calling all movers, shakers, and daiquiri dream-makers: your mission is simple…
Make the best daiquiri. While blindfolded. In 60 seconds. In front of a crowd. No pressure.
After almost four years of shaking, stirring, caffeinating, feeding, and occasionally corrupting Wellington, Elixir Cocktail Apotheke is throwing a glorious, FINAL 𝐁𝐋𝐈𝐍𝐃𝐅𝐎𝐋𝐃𝐄𝐃 𝐃𝐀𝐈𝐐𝐔𝐈𝐑𝐈 𝐂𝐎𝐌𝐏𝐄𝐓𝐈𝐓𝐈𝐎𝐍 before we ride off into the cocktail sunset.
This year, we are pairing up with 𝐃𝐢𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐨 𝐑𝐮𝐦, courtesy of 𝐇𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐨𝐜𝐤𝐬 AND the 𝐖𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠𝐭𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐜𝐤𝐭𝐚𝐢𝐥 𝐂𝐥𝐮𝐛
https://www.facebook.com/groups/246872129390986
Competitors will be given four mystery spirits, juices, and sugars in unmarked bottles, pick their fight song, then step up and shake like the future of cocktails depends on it.
Expect some Elixir closing-party nonsense: specials galore, questionable games, family pricing, and memories to last.
There’ll be laughter, probably some tears, and definitely some questionable dance moves. This is our last hurrah — one more night to raise a glass, hail Satan, and make some chaos together.

𝐖𝐞 𝐫𝐞𝐩𝐞𝐚𝐭, 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧 𝐮𝐩𝐬 𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐬𝐨𝐨𝐧, 𝐬𝐨 𝐤𝐞𝐞𝐩 𝐚 𝐠𝐨𝐝𝐝𝐚𝐦𝐧 𝐜𝐥𝐨𝐬𝐞 𝐞𝐲𝐞 𝐨𝐧 𝐭𝐡𝐢𝐬 𝐩𝐚𝐠𝐞.
𝑭𝒆𝒓𝒏𝒆𝒕 𝒊𝒔 𝒃𝒊𝒕𝒕𝒆𝒓,
𝑪𝒖𝒓𝒂𝒄𝒂𝒐 𝒎𝒊𝒈𝒉𝒕 𝒃𝒆 𝒃𝒍𝒖𝒆,

name: Thyme Well Spent: Bubbles & Breads
venue: 1023 Longbush Road, Masterton
long_description:
Nestle into the warmth of the Longbush Cottage glasshouse for a mindful winter afternoon of watercolour nature journaling. Surrounded by the subtle beauty of the winter garden, you’ll be guided to sketch and paint seasonal herbs like rosemary, thyme, and sage, learning techniques to capture detail and texture in your journal. This peaceful session is perfect for slowing down and reconnecting with nature through art.
Sip on bubbles and savour warm rosemary focaccia with herb-infused oil as you work. No experience required- just bring a curious eye and a love of seasonal beauty.
Date & Time: Saturday 16th August | 2-4pm
Location: 1023 Longbush Road, Longbush 5884
(20 min scenic drive from Martinborough)
Price: $119

id: Life Drawing @ Wellington Naturist Club-20 Molloys Road, Upper Hutt-Facebook
name: Life Drawing @ Wellington Naturist Club
venue: 20 Molloys Road, Upper Hutt
long_description:
Life drawing with a difference - it's Life Drawing @ Wellington Naturist Club. The next one is on Sunday 17th Aug 2025.
We have the sessions monthly. If the weather is not suitable for outdoor life drawing then we'll be inside, nice and warm.
The fee for life drawing is the normal Day Visit Fee of $20, for non-members. (The life drawing is otherwise free for Club members and for non-members who are paying for an overnight stay on the Sat or Sun nights.)
Bring some lunch. Morning and afternoon tea are provided. You can bring your own art materials, or you can use some that are provided. Come to either draw or to model or swap between the two. All the models are volunteers, so a nice variety of shapes and sizes all in the one session. Draw if you like. Model if you like.
The session format: beginning the session with 2 minute poses to warm-up, then increasing the pose lengths to something between 10-30 minutes. Each model posing chooses how long their pose(s) will be in that 10-30 minute range.
Some tuition is given to those who would like it. We'll be life drawing from 11am until 3pm, but you’re very welcome to come and go as you please, and to use the club facilities. It's a pretty casual event.
There's full kitchen facilities, BBQ facilities, the new hot tub, spa, sauna, table tennis, golf, eels to feed, bush walks etc. Make a day of it if you like.
If you're coming for the life drawing at the club, then the only time anyone is obliged to wear "club uniform" is if you're modelling for the life drawing, or you’re using the spa/hot tub/sauna/pool. Otherwise just wear - or don't wear - what you like. No need to book either, just come enjoy the day.
Wellington Naturist Club, 20 Molloys Rd, Te Marua, Upper Hutt 5018. Call the club mobile 027 330 6212 when you get to the gate, so you can be buzzed in. Then follow the signs to park and come up to the hall to see where we will be having the session.

id: The Magnificent Weirdos Presents "First Draft" - Eight Rehearsed Readings of Plays in Development-12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
name: The Magnificent Weirdos Presents "First Draft" - Eight Rehearsed Readings of Plays in Development
venue: 12 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington
long_description:
From 15-17 August, The Magnificent Weirdos is teaming up with writers in the early stages of creating new work to present rehearsed readings of these works in progress for the public, followed by audience kōrero with each writer/creator.
All the work is created by writers and devisers with lived experience of disability, neurodivergence, d/Deafness or mental distress.
Each work of approximately 20 minutes will be presented once over the weekend. Each reading will be followed by a 30 minute chat with the writer, facilitated by the director. It's an exciting and creative way to develop new work, both for writers, who receive feedback at an early stage, and for audiences who get to water the seed from which the fruit will eventually grow.
We will also be hosting an industry workshop led by Playmarket, leading a conversation on opportunities, processes and accessibility for new playwrights. Playwrights will need to register for this free workshop which has 20 spaces.
The whole event is NZSL interpreted. Accessible to blind/low vision folks without audio description.
Check out the program for more details of each play, content notes and pre show information here: https://www.magnificentweirdos.com/fd-program
#FirstDraft #Playwrights #LiveTheatre #Playreading #NeurodivergentWriters #DeafWriters #DisabledWriters #HannahPlayhouse #Wellington
INSTAGRAM:
@hannahplayhouse @sameena.zehra @WgtnCC @playmarketnz

id: Intro to Musical Improv | A PopRox Improv Course-61 - 69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington-Facebook
name: Intro to Musical Improv | A PopRox Improv Course
venue: 61 - 69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington
long_description:
Musical improv can be absolute magic. Whether it's a whole musical show, or single song that bursts out of an emotional scene, music adds so much to our improv.
This course for improvisers will give an introduction to musical improv. We'll cover basic song structures, how to get into a song from a scene, how to support a song through chorus work, and why and when we use music to add to our improv.
Participants should be confident in scene-work and have at least 2 years improv experience. This course will not teach you how to approach scenes and characters, so you should have a solid foundation which you can add to with this new toolbox of musical skills.
The course includes 6 workshops which will run 6:30pm to 8:30pm on Wednesdays from August 6th to September 10th. As it is an intro course, we do not anticipate a showcase for this course.
ABOUT THE TEACHERS
LIA KELLY is a theatre-maker and teacher based in Pōneke. She is an established director, actor, improviser and musician and has been a part of productions across the city over the last 10 years. She directed One Night Band which won 'Most Original Production' at the Wellington Theatre Awards 2023, and performs regularly as both a player and musician for POPROX.
ISAAC THOMAS has been an improviser and improv musician for a decade in Pōneke and Whakatū/Nelson. He's played with Body in Space, Playshop, Poprox and been musical director for musical improv powerhouse The Pāua Ballads. He has worked with almost every major improv company around the city accompanying them through improvised piano, guitar and vocals.
THE STANDY-BY LIST
PopRox firmly believes that improv is for everyone, especially when it comes to sharing skills and knowledge. So we're offering "the stand-by list" for all our workshops in 2025. We'll giveaway any spots remaining a few days before the first workshop of the course, on a first-come-first-serve basis. No need to provide a reason, no questions asked. If the entry fee is a barrier for you to come along, then simply email poproximprov@gmail.com and we'll pop you on the SBL. Everyone on the list will be notified whether there was a place available for them 3 days before the first workshop.

id: Mulled Wine Concert - ERNA FERRYwith the Rodger FOX Jazz Ensemble-62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Mulled Wine Concert - ERNA FERRYwith the Rodger FOX Jazz Ensemble
venue: 62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand
long_description:
REMEMBERING A JAZZ GREAT
ERNA FERRY AND RODGER FOX JAZZ ENSEMBLE TO PLAY THE WAIRARAPA
Jazzy grooves with Horns; Erna Ferry, partner of the late Rodger Fox and long-time
singer with his band describes the show she will be presenting for Mulled Wine Concerts in
Featherston at 4:00 PM on Saturday 16 August 2025.
Rodger was a great jazzman and teacher and put together jazz groups of all sizes, ranging from his famed Big Band to
smaller combos more suited to more intimate venues.
He has left us, but the jazz he championed so well remains. The musicians who were associated with him still want to
play and I still want to sing. Together with the principal musicians from Rodger line-up, she will cover an eclectic range of songs from The Beatles to Beyond for Wairarapa audiences at the Featherston event.
Mary Gow, the organiser of the concert reminds us that Rodger Fox and his ensemble played several times in Anzac Hall and he loved the informality and passion for jazz he found in the area.
We were so happy to learn that this group will carry on the good work she notes. It won't be a sad occasion at all, but a happy celebration of the good-time music and fun he enjoyed
The Mulled Wine Concerts organisation has been presenting musical events for sixteen years, ranging from full-on classical events - piano recitals, string quartets and soloists - through Celtic folk music and ukeleles, and also jazz and band music. Over the years Gow's focus on quality has cemented her series of concerts into must-see happenings and whatever the genre of music is presented, they usually attract a good audience.
The popularity of jazz makes it certain that the 16 August concert will be well attended!
===ENDS===
Mulled Wine Concerts presents Erna Ferry and the Rodger Fox Jazz Ensemble
at the ANZAC Hall, Bell Street, Featherston
Saturday 16 August at 4:00 PM
Tickets $25 (Student under 16, $10). Online Sales: marygow@gmail.com
Ticket outlets: Dickensian Bookshop, Featherston; iSite Martinborough and Masterton; Mrs
Blackwells Village Bookshop, 93 Main Street, Greytown.
Info: www.mulledwineconcerts.com

id: Mexican Prehispanic Experience at AWHI-34 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Mexican Prehispanic Experience at AWHI
venue: 34 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Join Claudio in his Mexican project called Xochipilli´s Eye. Xochipilli (Pronounce: Zo-chee-pee-lee) is the Mexica god of music, flowers, arts, and sacred plants.
Claudio´s last trip home to Mexico was full of inspiration, reconnecting him to his ancestral roots in Mexico City. He was fortunate to meet a prehispanic musician and instrument maker who plays and crafts beautiful traditional instruments. Claudio returned to NZ with many special new instruments; and continues to be inspired and guided by the cultural wealth, wisdom, and knowledge of the ancient cultures of the Americas.
This event will be extraordinary and unique. You will learn about Mexican prehispanic cosmology and spirituality. Experience simple and powerful rituals together with unique musical instruments used by Olmecs, Toltecs, Aztecs, and Mayans. The instruments include clay flutes, clay drums, medicine drums, ocarinas, wooden flutes, shells, and shakers skilfully arranged to create magical shamanic atmospheres.
We will be sharing Cacao which is an energetic traditional Mexican prehispanic drinks that help open the heart, reconnect with the earth element, ignite your fire, and get you up and moving.
What to expect:
Cleansing copal and herbal welcoming.
Talk about the use of music in the ancient times.
An opening ceremony to acknowledge the Tlalmanalli (offering and altar) with the deep sound of the conch shell in the four directions.
Cacao ceremony: Claudio will offer the cacao drink as a connection with the earth, to yourself, and the moment.
Live music that invokes many emotions and imagery, from contemplative natural and mystical environments to uplifting and empowering rhythms. You are free to project and explore within and/ or express and liberate projecting outwards.
We will finish with a shamanic atmosphere relaxation and close the event with a traditional chant.
No previous experience is required.
What to bring:
Wear comfortable clothing! Bring an open heart and a mind ready to learn and receive. You are also welcome to bring a fruit and or a flower to contribute to the altar.
Date & Time: Saturday, April 13th, 6-7:30pm
Location: AWHI Yoga & Wellbeing, Level 2, 15 Johnston Street, Wellington Central
Investment: $55

id: Chloe's Revenge-1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Chloe's Revenge
venue: 1 Kent Ter, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Written and performed by Chloe Bettina.
Part love letter, part exposé, Chloe’s Revenge is a musical theatre-inspired show that pulls back the curtain on the pro-am musical theatre “industry” in Aotearoa. With biting wit, Chloe lays bare the heartbreak of rejection from the world she adores, and turns that pain into power. This is a story of survival, self-discovery, and the ultimate comeback: becoming the best version of yourself when the system says you don’t belong. You don't have to be a "musical theatre person" to enjoy this show. In fact, it's better if you're not.

id: Cringeworthy: The 90's-1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Cringeworthy: The 90's
venue: 1A Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Cringeworthy – 90s!
A high-octane explosion of ‘90s nostalgia!
Written and devised by Andrea Sanders
Produced by Beatgirl Productions
Starring Andrea Sanders, Devon Neiman, Jared Pallesen and Emma Katene
Following the success of Cringeworthy ’60s, ’70s and ’80s, this show pays homage to NZ music and culture in the last decade of the 20th century. Expect a ‘rad’ mix of local and international hits delivered with snappy dance moves, scintillating vocals and a generous dollop of cheesy comedy.
The 90s was a decade bursting with diverse musical genres, from boy bands and girl groups to MMMBop and hip hop, the swing revival (Mambo No. 5), country (Achy Breaky Heart), The Macarena and re-releases of iconic hits such as To Sir With Love, Silly Love Songs, Unchained Melody and Get Down on It (can you dig it??).
On the local scene, legends such as Crowded House, Dave Dobbyn, The Exponents, Margaret Urlich, Supergroove, True Bliss, Ardijah, South Side of Bombay and The Chills gave us heavenly pop hits that have become Kiwi classics.
In some ‘fly’ outfits, Cringeworthy – the ’90s packages the hits of the decade (including Can’t Touch This, Weather With You, Love Shack and How Bizarre) with some unforgettable fashion trends including flannel shirts, baggy jeans, tracksuits and chokers. These energetic styles are woven together with a witty narrative reflecting the lifestyle, news events and culture of the time – smelling like Teen Spirit!
What’s the time Mr Wolf? Time to grab your ticket to ride this retro rollercoaster! Nostalgia invites you to relive a decade that still resonates today. Get ready to party like its 1999!

I’ve written some songs for piano so I’ll have my keyboard with me, plus my looper for adding drums, harmonies and lead guitar - the full worksburger! Looking forward to playing my old and new songs for you
. It’s a small cosy venue so best to book a table (come for dinner - the food is delish) to secure a seat. Free entry - with a koha tin collection. x
Steph Casey’s debut album charted in the NZ top ten and she released her second album to glowing international reviews. Her songs are primarily indie-folk/americana in genre, with her most recent work embracing more grit and distorted guitar, as her sound evolves. Her songs have over half a million plays across platforms, and were licensed for the award-winning feature film ‘Poppy’.
She has featured on TV3’s The Cafe, in Next magazine, and on RNZ’s NZ Live.
“This second album by the acclaimed indie-folk singer Steph Casey confirms a strong and unique talent.”
– Graham Reid, NZ Herald

id: Return Season! The Magic of Her Body: Readings of Katherine Mansfield's Queer Writing-25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Return Season! The Magic of Her Body: Readings of Katherine Mansfield's Queer Writing
venue: 25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Back after a sold-out season during the 2025 Wellington Pride Festival!
"She enthrals me, enslaves me – and her personal self – her body absolute – is my worship."
Heritage New Zealand Pouhere Taonga, Katherine Mansfield House & Garden, and Vixen Temple present The Magic of Her Body: Readings of Katherine Mansfield’s Queer Writing, an immersive theatrical experience in one of Aotearoa’s leading historic house museums.
Join performance artist Vixen Temple and historian Kerryn Pollock of Heritage New Zealand Pouhere Taonga on a journey through Mansfield’s sensual diary entries and sapphic-coded short stories, moving through the rooms in the home of her birth, Katherine Mansfield House & Garden in Thorndon. Live musical accompaniment will be performed by Ms Weeds.
All details and booking via www.katherinemansfield.com

id: Mana Noctourno-18 Raiha St, Elsdon, Porirua-Facebook
name: Mana Noctourno
venue: 18 Raiha St, Elsdon, Porirua
long_description:
The mana cycle group and the wmtbc are pleased to invite you to the inaugural mana noctourno.
The race is a more social vibe with 3 optional stages that you can race at your leisure, its all about having fun and racing your mates!
The three stages are:
Trail 10/Chimney
Tumeke
Blaster
You can race 1 stage or all the stages as many times as you like, there's no official winner.
MANDATORY EQUIPMENT
You must have two fully charged night lights, one mounted to your handlebars and one mounted to your helmet, each with 1000 Lumens or more and a NZ Safety Standards approved helmet.

RACE TIMETABLE
4:00pm Registration
4:45pm: Mandatory rider briefing
5pm: Race start
9pm: Race finish
Online registration closes Friday 15th August, 9pm. Entries will be limited to 150.
ON-THE-DAY PRICES
There may be limited on the day entries. Registering online is highly recommended to speed things up on the day and save on registration. Entry prices on the day will be $40 (members) and $60 (non-members).
TRAVELLING TO THE NOCTOURNO
The event village will be located at the grassy park at Camp Elsdon/Raiha St carpark.
REFUNDS AND CANCELLATION
In the event that you are no longer able to attend (and notify us by the Friday prior to the event) or the event is canceled due to bad weather or unforeseen circumstances – we are unable to provide refunds, so instead we will credit your WMTBC account. You can use the credit to pay for future events, WMTBC membership or as a donation to Wellington trails.
EVENT TERMS AND CONDITIONS
NOTE: If you are under 18 years of age, you need your parent or guardian to sign/agree on your behalf.
I agree to wear a NZ Safety Standards approved helmet for the duration of any runs down the tracks.
I accept the risks inherent in mountain biking and will not hold the event organisers or landowner liable for any damages.
I am personally responsible for ensuring my bike is securely on the shuttle transport (if part of this event) and will not hold the Wellington Mountain Bike Club Inc liable to any damage.
If you need to stop on a trail, pull to the side and ensure both you and your bike are well off the trail.
You may injure yourself or come across an injured rider, if it is serious, someone should remain stationary with the injured rider and then the next rider must notify a marshal as soon as possible.
I agree to abide by all rules set out by the Wellington Mountain bike Club Inc.
I agree to a fee of $100 in the event that the race timing token allocated to me is not returned to WMTBC at the end of the event.
All entrants must have two night lights, one helmet mounted and one handlebar mounted, each with a minimum of 1000 Lumens.

id: The Life of Henry the Fifth-1 Kent Terrace, Wellington-Facebook
name: The Life of Henry the Fifth
venue: 1 Kent Terrace, Wellington
long_description:
A young man, once revelling in freedom and reckless camaraderie, is thrust into the role of a king, burdened with the fate of England.
Victory or ruin rests on his shoulders, yet his newfound power isolates him, leaving him lost, uncertain, and deeply unsettled.
Betrayed by friends, mistrusted by his men, and taunted by those who seek his downfall, Henry’s journey is both severe and profoundly relatable.
Shakespeare crafts a world where soldiers fight without knowing why, where the battlefield sees vagrants and children stand shoulder to shoulder, where uncertain rulers navigate a landscape devoid of guidance from the past. It is a world that, despite its historical setting, feels unnervingly close to our own.
With only ten dynamic performers, this is Henry the Fifth like never before: stripped back, sharp, and driven by the raw intensity of Shakespeare’s text.
Expect fast-paced storytelling, immersive staging, and a production that puts the audience at the heart of the battle.
Full show duration: 95 min including a 15 min intermission.
“This production epitomises what can be achieved when talent, teamwork, hard work, opportunity and passion combine.’”
NZ Herald on The Tragedy of Macbeth

id: Evening meeting - AGM; Tony Druce Memorial Lecture-101 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Evening meeting - AGM; Tony Druce Memorial Lecture
venue: 101 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Speakers: Ilse Breitwieser, Research Associate – Botanist; Rob Smissen, Senior Researcher – Botanist. Allan Herbarium, Manaaki Whenua – Landcare Research, Lincoln.
Call them billy-buttons, drumstick flowers, billy balls, sun balls (in Australia) or woollyheads and puatea in New Zealand, or with their scientific name Craspedia (Gnaphalieae, Compositae/Asteraceae), these everlasting daisies are conspicuous members of many plant communities in New Zealand and Australia but remain an outstanding taxonomic challenge.
In 1961, based on a small number of available herbarium specimens, HH Allan’s Flora of New Zealand volume 1 recorded just 6 species in New Zealand. However, the 1992 and 1993 checklists of Tony Druce, who made extensive field observations and collected numerous herbarium specimens, distinguished more than 45 undescribed entities that might warrant taxonomic recognition.
Morphological variation in New Zealand Craspedia is complex, making the definition and circumscription of species problematic. At least in part, this difficulty is the legacy of an extremely rapid and recent diversification of the genus in New Zealand—a scenario that produces challenges for genetic as well as morphological approaches to delimiting species.

id: Jenny Mitchell | The Forest House Tour | Wellington-Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Jenny Mitchell | The Forest House Tour | Wellington
venue: Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Presented by Strung Out Touring
Jenny Mitchell - The Forest House Tour
After opening for Kasey Chambers around Australia, award-winning songwriter Jenny Mitchell returns home for a national tour celebrating her acclaimed album Forest House.
An Aotearoa Music Award Winner and 2 x Australian Golden Guitar nominee, Jenny is a gifted storyteller from the deep south of New Zealand. Now based in Melbourne, the AU Review describes Jenny’s music as a “sublime slice of folk” and her new highly anticipated album, Forest House, is capturing fans across the motu and the globe.
“Forest House - 10/10,” Newstalk ZB
"Every so often, an artist emerges who has everything it takes to reach the pinnacle of country music success. Jenny Mitchell is a star.” Country Music Capital News
Jenny is thrilled to welcome very special guest Katie Thompson to The Forest House Tour. Hailing from New Zealand’s West Coast, Katie is a captivating alt-country artist known for her soul-stirring storytelling and rich, emotive voice. A 2024 finalist for Best Country Song, Katie has shared stages with legends like Sir Elton John and Don McLean, leaving lasting impressions with her raw honesty and polished edge.
WELLINGTON
Old St Paul's
Sunday 18 August
Doors open at 5pm
Support from Katie Thompson
NZ TOUR DETAILS
SAT 17 AUGUST - Errick's Venue, Dunedin
SUN 18 AUGUST - Old St Paul's, Wellington
THURS 21 AUGUST - The Piano, Christchurch
FRI 22 AUGUST - The Tuning Fork, Auckland
For more information please visit www.jennymitchell.co.nz.
ABOUT JENNY MITCHELL
NZ Best Country Artist Winner and 2 x Golden Guitar nominee, Jenny Mitchell, is a storyteller from the deep south of New Zealand with impactful songs full of grace and grit. Her last album ‘Tug of War’ included the pivotal release of the 2022 APRA NZ Best Country Song ‘Trouble Finds a Girl’ and was described by Rolling Stone as an album, “worthy of every award it will inevitably collect.”
No stranger to the road, Jenny was recently awarded a scholarship to perform at Nashville’s iconic AmericanaFest and has toured Australia and New Zealand extensively over the last decade - supporting artists including Fanny Lumsden (AU), Tami Neilson (NZ) and Nadia Reid (NZ) along the way. Her most recent touring project, The Bush & the Birds Tour, travelled throughout NZ in support of conservation organisation; Forest & Bird. An ode to her late Grandfather’s love of the land, the tour generated over $2000 in donations for Forest & Bird.
Jenny grew up in a home and family of music and often tours with her sisters, The Mitchell Twins, and country music loving Dad, Ron Mitchell. After growing up with a musical diet of The Chicks, Hank Williams and Dolly Parton, Jenny penned her first songs at the age of 11 after a trip to Ireland where she connected with her Irish heritage and explored the immigration story of her Irish born Grandparents. A few years down the track, Jenny performed original work on the TV show New Zealand's Got Talent, was placed 3rd and went onto crowdfund and release her first album at the age of 15. In 2017, she was crowned the overall winner of the NZ Gold Guitar Awards which kickstarted the professional chapter of her career, leading to her Aotearoa Music Award Winning album 'Wildfires’ produced by Tasmania’s Matt Fell.
With a sound that No Depression describes as, “poetry that dominates a beautiful melody,” Jenny Mitchell is comfortable with a country twang or softly spoken ballad and is rapidly capturing fans across the globe.

id: Mulled Wine Concert - Erna Ferry with the Rodger Fox JAZZ Ensemble-62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Mulled Wine Concert - Erna Ferry with the Rodger Fox JAZZ Ensemble
venue: 62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand
long_description:
REMEMBERING A JAZZ GREAT
ERNA FERRY AND RODGER FOX JAZZ ENSEMBLE TO PLAY THE WAIRARAPA
"Jazzy grooves with Horns" Erna Ferry, partner of the late Rodger Fox and long-time
singer with his band describes the show she will be presenting for Mulled Wine Concerts in
Featherston at 4:00 PM on Saturday 16 August 2025. "Rodger was a great jazzman and
teacher and put together jazz groups of all sizes, ranging from his famed Big Band to
smaller combos more suited to more intimate venues. He has left us, but the jazz he
championed so well remains. The musicians who were associated with him still want to
play and I still want to sing". Together with the principal musicians from Rodger's line-up,
she will cover an eclectic range of songs from "The Beatles to Beyond" for Wairarapa
audiences at the Featherston event.
Mary Gow, the organiser of the concert reminds us that Rodger Fox and his ensemble
played several times in Anzac Hall and he loved the informality and passion for jazz he
found in the area. "We were so happy to learn that this group will carry on the good work"
she notes. "It won't be a sad occasion at all, but a happy celebration of the good-time
music and fun he enjoyed".
The Mulled WIne Concerts organisation has been presenting musical events for sixteen
years, ranging from full-on classical events - piano recitals, string quartets and soloists -
through Celtic folk music and ukeleles, and also jazz and band music. Over the years
Gow's focus on quality has cemented her series of concerts into must-see happenings and
whatever the genre of music is presented, they usually attract a good audience. The
popularity of jazz makes it certain that the 16 August concert will be well attended!
===ENDS===
Mulled Wine Concerts presents Erna Ferry and the Rodger Fox Jazz Ensemble
at the ANZAC Hall, Bell Street, Featherston
Saturday 16 August at 4:00 PM
Tickets $25 (Student under 16, $10). Online Sales: marygow@gmail.com
Ticket outlets: Dickensian Bookshop, Featherston; iSite Martinborough and Masterton; Mrs
Blackwell's Village Bookshop, 93 Main Street, Greytown.
Info: www.mulledwineconcerts.com

name: Songwriter Showcase NZ #105
venue: 129 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
long_description:
Wonderful turn out at our last showcase - thank you to everyone for the effort on these cold wet nights! #105 on the way - Sunday August 17th - another exciting line up!
The Faustians
With the banjo, fiddle, guitar and bass, and the wailing vocals of their Oldtime Appalachian roots, The Faustians have veered off course into a strange world of lost souls, bleak plains and cries for help from the dystopian dreamscapes of Helena’s original song writing.
Beans is a recent fixture in the Pōneke folk scene, importing a wild
array of original songs and generally embodying the classic singer
songwriter formula – introvert plus guitar. They gained international acclaim in 2021 as a finalist in the Liverpool International Song Contest, and have since performed at small venues and festivals across Australia and Aotearoa. They are also one third of folk trio 'Don & the Divorcees', but are known for solo shows which are rich with humour, honesty and interesting word choices. Expect to feel things.
Martin Andrews is a singer/songwriter from Pōneke, Aotearoa. He writes blues and country based songs of humanity, mixing story telling with lived experience. For this show he will be playing songs from his upcoming fourth album “Hands” and will be joined by long time friend and collaborator, Scott Pilgrim.
Billy Lyrical has been writing, singing, busking, mixing, composing and performing for over 40 years. Like a mix of Tom Waits, Tom Lehrer and Woody Guthrie, he's written hundreds of songs and played thousands of gigs. He's not on Facebook, Instagram, Tiktok, Twitter, Linkedin, Spotify, Tumblr, Tindr, Grindr, Onlyfans or Pornhub. He's alive and kicking, living in the moment, rocking in the pocket, riffing this condition. We're now entering an Extinction Event, Please extinguish your cigarettes.

More details to come shortly - see you there!!

id: Daffodil Express 2025-Bunny Street, Wellington-Facebook
name: Daffodil Express 2025
venue: Bunny Street, Wellington
long_description:
Carterton’s Daffodil Festival in the early Spring creates a great day out for the whole family. Why not travel there in style by a heritage train, let Steam Incorporated take the stress out of travel as you take the train to the Wairarapa. This year, our train has reduced capacity, so be sure to book in quickly!
Hauled by our heritage locomotives, take a glimpse into the bygone era of train travel as we clear the rush of Wellington, racing through the suburbs of Petone, Lower Hutt & Upper Hutt. After picking up the last of our passengers at Upper Hutt, we will race through the 8.8km long Rimutaka tunnel. This tunnel remains the longest in New Zealand with scheduled passenger trains, and has just been subjected to a full track relay for the first time since it was opened in 1955.
Passing through Featherston, we head east and navigate the rolling hills towards Carterton.
Arriving into Carterton, you'll have a few hours to disembark and go and explore Carterton's annual Daffodil Festival , before jumping on the train for the return journey home.
The Daffodil Fields and Festival
Located in the heart of the Wairarapa, Carterton’s popular Daffodil Festival offers an abundance of activities ranging from the famous Daffodil picking at Middlerun Farm, markets along High and Holloway Streets, the Big Wai Art sale in the events Centre, live music, and loads more family fun.
Shuttle buses will run from the town centre to the daffodil fields. There will be a lot of people for these buses, so please be patient. The bus transfer is free.
Here you can pick your own daffodils for $5 (Cash Preferred) that is a fundraiser for Wellington Free Ambulance and Whānau Āwhina Plunket, Wairarapa region. While stocks last!
The fields are not suitable for walkers or wheelchairs due to uneven grounds.
Prior booking is essential, this is an annual sell out event!
Fares
Adult (13+) - $125
Child (2-12yrs) - $90
Infant - Free
For more information & tickets, head to https://steaminc.org.nz/book-train-trip/daffodil-express/

id: The Art of Mending-157 Hataitai Rd, Hataitai, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
name: The Art of Mending
venue: 157 Hataitai Rd, Hataitai, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
long_description:
This class is cared for by the whimsical Aisha, a multidisciplinary artist with a passion for empowering the mending process. Each month on Wednesday evenings Aisha will introduce us to a different technique for reviving our beloved textile pieces. If you're like us and have a box of clothing in need of repairs that never seems to empty... this is the club for you. With three events a month we'll inspire each other to creatively care for those pieces we love! This club is also perfect for anyone who loves learning new skills. Techniques we'll cover are lino printing, patching, darning, felting, embroidery, beading, sashiko, fabric painting and more! All materials and tools are supplied at each class - but you are welcome to bring your own and we highly encourage bringing a textile item (or many!) to fix up.
In August we're lino printing!
Class 01: 13th August
In this class we'll get an introduction to lino printing on fabric and best practice for using this process to cover stains. Pre cut designs and all other tools are supplied. Please bring either clothing you would like to print on or some fabric scrap to make lino printed patches!
Class 02: 20th August
At this class Aisha will take us through the process of designing and cutting our own lino to print with! You're welcome to keep going with the pre cut designs as well. Lino and carving tools will be supplied.
Class 03: 27th August
This class will be open to what you want to work on! Bring in larger project items, print with the lino cuts you made in the previous week, or cut some fresh ones!

long_description:

PINBALL TOURNAMENT AT YE OLDE PINBALL SHOPPE
Pōneke's Premier Pinball Tournament for your future career as an internationally ranked pinball player!
Make a hearty pilgrimage through the arcade, trying your hand at a selection of our finely curated games, report your scores to the staff, and watch your ranking on the tournament ladder rise as your scores are stacked up against your competitors. Not happy with a score you got? No problem, you can keep trying until you're
happy with it!
Make it into the Top 16 before the end of Saturday, and you'll be invited to play in the finals round the following Sunday, where 1st - 8th will play in A Division, and 9th - 16th will play in B Division.
This finals round is free of charge, and comes with a whopping prize pool worth $200 in value awarded to the top finishers in each division.
So what are you waiting for? Come and show us your skills!
Qualification Round (10th - 16th August):
Each player is granted access to a token deal of 12 tokens for $20!
- Sunday to Saturday
- Submit at least one score on each of the selected 8 games during open hours.
- Your best score on each game is ranked.
- Take a picture of your score and submit it at the counter.
- Unlimited qualifying! Not satisfied with your score? Grab more tokens and keep playing to improve it!
Scoring System:
- Linear Scoring applies. Score 100pts for the top score on each selected game, 99pts for 2nd, 98pts for 3rd, and so on.
Finals (13th July):
Top 16 qualifiers will battle it out on Sunday from 12pm onwards, with 8 in A-Division, and 8 in B-Division! Keep an eye on our socials if you've participated in the competition throughout the week - those who have qualified for the finals will be announced on SATURDAY night (the 12th).
You could win a share of $200 in Shoppe Credit!
How to Participate:
Simply come to Ye Olde Pinball Shoppe during open hours and submit your scores from Monday to Saturday!

Big Guns
D&D

Jack Bot

Led Zeppelin
Twilight Zone

id: ERNA FERRY with the Zanzibar Jazz Ensemble - Former Rodger Fox Jazz Ensemble-62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand-Facebook
name: ERNA FERRY with the Zanzibar Jazz Ensemble - Former Rodger Fox Jazz Ensemble
venue: 62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand
long_description:
REMEMBERING A JAZZ GREAT
ERNA FERRY AND RODGER FOX JAZZ ENSEMBLE TO PLAY THE WAIRARAPA
"Jazzy grooves with Horns" Erna Ferry, partner of the late Rodger Fox and long-time singer with his band describes the show she will be presenting for Mulled Wine Concerts in Featherston at 4:00 PM on Saturday 16 August 2025. "Rodger was a great jazzman and teacher and put together jazz groups of all sizes, ranging from his famed Big Band to smaller combos more suited to more intimate venues. He has left us, but the jazz he championed so well remains. The musicians who were associated with him still want to play and I still want to sing". Together with the principal musicians from Rodger's line-up, she will cover an eclectic range of songs from "The Beatles to Beyond" for Wairarapa audiences at the Featherston event.
Mary Gow, the organiser of the concert reminds us that Rodger Fox and his ensemble played several times in Anzac Hall and he loved the informality and passion for jazz he found in the area. "We were so happy to learn that this group will carry on the good work" she notes. "It won't be a sad occasion at all, but a happy celebration of the good-time music and fun he enjoyed".
The Mulled WIne Concerts organisation has been presenting musical events for sixteen years, ranging from full-on classical events - piano recitals, string quartets and soloists - through Celtic folk music and ukeleles, and also jazz and band music. Over the years Gow's focus on quality has cemented her series of concerts into must-see happenings and whatever the genre of music is presented, they usually attract a good audience. The popularity of jazz makes it certain that the 16 August concert will be well attended!
===ENDS===
Mulled Wine Concerts presents Erna Ferry and the Rodger Fox Jazz Ensemble
at the ANZAC Hall, Bell Street, Featherston
Saturday 16 August at 4:00 PM
Tickets $25 (Student under 16, $10). Online Sales: marygow@gmail.com
Ticket outlets: Dickensian Bookshop, Featherston; iSite Martinborough and Masterton; Mrs Blackwell's Village Bookshop, 93 Main Street, Greytown.
Info: www.mulledwineconcerts.com

• Bring a bag, as you must take away leftover harakeke

id: Embody Your Flow - Reconnect to the wisdom of your menstrual cycle-7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Embody Your Flow - Reconnect to the wisdom of your menstrual cycle
venue: 7 Colombo St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
long_description:
Explore how to recognise your body’s rhythms, needs and strengths throughout your menstrual cycle, and discover supportive practices grounded in somatic and embodied awareness.
Attuning to the wisdom of the menstrual cycle offers a meaningful way to honour your natural rhythms, nurturing ease, creativity, and deeper connection.
This course is trauma-informed and inclusive. No prior somatics experience is necessary, and all who menstruate are welcome.
****************
Bookings:
https://www.trybooking.com/nz/events/landing/16754?embed
Or email amyeliserichards@gmail.com
Ticket prices range from $50-$75. If finance is a barrier please reach out.
****************
Amy Richards is a yoga teacher based in Pōneke, with a Certificate in MindBody Therapy. She is drawn to the ways intentional movement and embodied awareness can shape our inner experience and relationships. Grounded in a trauma-informed approach, her work invites exploration of connection with sensation and emotion through the body.
Her practice is influenced by Tantric Shaivism, contact improvisation, Open Floor dance, Hakomi somatic psychotherapy, Somatic Experiencing, Authentic Relating, and Nonviolent Communication.

Photo credit: Image by pikisuperstar on Freepik

venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Sound Healing Experience with Claudio Escutia
It’ll be a transcendent evening of Sacred Harmonic Sounds, where multi-instrumentalist Claudio Escutia will guide you through a deeply restorative sonic experience. Plus - there will be an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert included in your ticket!
Let the vibrations of Gongs, Crystal and Tibetan singing bowls, Native American flutes, and other ancient instruments wash over you—melting away tension, quieting the mind, and opening a doorway to inner stillness.
With Claudio’s masterful soundscapes, you’ll not only hear the music but feel it resonating through your body, inviting profound relaxation and a reconnection to your heart. No prior meditation or yoga experience is needed—just come as you are and let the sound carry you into a space of deep peace.
What to Bring?
Yoga mats, blankets & cushions are provided but you are also welcome to bring your own yoga mat and any extra cushions or any other comforts for the ultimate experience!
Why You Won’t Want to Miss This
A rare opportunity to experience Claudio’s unique fusion of world music traditions and healing sound
A deeply immersive, meditative experience—perfect for relaxation and renewal
Limited spaces
Meet Claudio Escutia, your host
Born into a musical family in Mexico City, Claudio’s lifelong passion for sound has taken him across the globe—from studying jazz in Montreal to mastering Tibetan singing bowls in Kathmandu. His music weaves together classical training, world instruments (Sitar, Esraj, Harmonium, and more), and a deep reverence for sound as medicine.
Ready to unwind and recharge through the magic of vibration?

id: Manawa Karioi Working Bees-7 Danube St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand-Facebook
name: Manawa Karioi Working Bees
venue: 7 Danube St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
long_description:
Our track maintenance and planting working bees are focussing on plant species diversity rather than large planting quantities. We have ordered 500 special trees from the Berhampore Nursery. We are in this for the long haul and a forest that contains a wide range of plant species will, in turn, attract a wider range of birds, lizards, and invertebrates. A wider range of plant species also allows for the forest to naturally regenerate and helps prevent weed species from establishing.
Tools are provided, bring sturdy shoes and a water bottle, Working bees are held every Sunday in June (except June 1st - Kings Birthday Weekend) and August 2025.
Meeting at the Information Shelter in the Tapu te Ranga Marae car park at the end of Danube Street in Island Bay. Children are welcome with parents or guardians.

name: Pre-Loved Clothing Market - Feral Nation Fundraiser

long_description:
FASHION FINDS: NEW & PRE-LOVED TREASURES

Shop for a Cause – Support Feral Nation!
Calling all bargain hunters, fashion lovers, and feline friends!
Join us for a one-day-only fashion market full of new and pre-loved clothing, designer labels, shoes, jewellery, and accessories – all at amazing prices.
Discover hidden fashion gems
Shop sustainably and keep clothes out of landfill
Save money and support a great cause!
This fabulous event is also a fundraiser for local cat rescue charity, Feral Nation.
All proceeds from stall booking fees go directly to helping Wellington’s stray and feral cats.
BONUS: Cute kittens will be onsite for cuddles!
Give a donation and enjoy some purr therapy while you browse.
**DISCLAIMER**
Please note that while we do our very best to ensure the presence of kittens for cuddles at this event, their attendance cannot be guaranteed. In the event of unforeseen circumstances such as illness, lack of available kittens, or other factors beyond our control, kitten cuddles may be limited or unavailable on the day. We appreciate your understanding and support of animal welfare, and thank you for your kindness and flexibility!
Date: Saturday, 16 August 2025
Time: 10am – 2pm
Location: Miramar & Maupuia Community Centre, 27 Chelsea Street, Miramar, Wellington
Want to sell? Got a wardrobe full of goodies to rehome?
Book a stall and turn your closet clean-out into cash for a cause!
APPLY HERE - https://forms.gle/t1KBk9TUArW6E4Cx9
Stalls cost $50 each for a 2m X 2m space (limited available)
Come for the bargains, stay for the kittens – we can’t wait to see you there!

id: KOHA SUNDAY YOGA - Heated Flow-34 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
name: KOHA SUNDAY YOGA - Heated Flow
venue: 34 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
long_description:
Join us EVERY Sunday for a 90 minute Heated Flow for koha entry (ie, give what you can)! Proceeds are donated to a different charitable organisation each month that's committed to supporting the wellbeing of Aotearoa!
This weekly class is open to everyone, regardless of yoga experience - haere mai!
BYO mat or hire for $2; towels available for $3 and make sure to bring a big water bottle!
To book: Download the AWHI app to view the timetable, or simply show up in good time on Sunday!
WHAT IS 'KOHA'? Koha loosely translates to 'gift' or exchange. In other words, pay what you can for the class!
For more information about our studio, visit our website at awhiyoga.co.nz
